beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and have coffee while listening to the rain on the roof.
beats.reasoning: Sofia wakes at home in Renton on a drizzly morning. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means she takes a slow, quiet start. The steady rain makes her want to linger indoors.

beats.activity: Drive to Downtown Seattle for a lunch/dinner shift at the restaurant, taking I-405 north to avoid the SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 is closed eastbound 9am–noon, so Sofia avoids it by taking I-405 north instead. She leaves early enough to miss the worst backup. The route is longer but safer than battling cross-lake traffic.

beats.activity: Prep for lunch service at the restaurant: set up station, review specials, hydrate.; Work lunch and early dinner service.
beats.reasoning: Sofia arrives early to the restaurant to prep her section before the lunch rush. Gives her time to settle in despite the rainy, low-energy morning.

beats.activity: Break at the restaurant: grab a sandwich and sit in the back, scroll through notes on new material.
beats.reasoning: Sofia takes a short break during the lull between lunch and dinner service. She uses the time to jot down comedy ideas sparked by customer interactions, staying in her head between rushes.
Evening service picks up as rain clears (2pm onward). Sofia pushes through a solid dinner rush, staying focused and efficient. No comedy show tonight—she's keeping this a restaurant day.

beats.activity: Drive home to Renton via I-405 south, avoiding lingering SR-520 delays.
beats.reasoning: Sofia leaves the restaurant after dinner service and takes I-405 south back to Renton. The SR-520 closure is lifting by now, but she sticks with her reliable route home.

beats.activity: Make a light dinner at home: leftover mushroom pasta and a glass of wine.
beats.reasoning: Sofia is tired after a long shift. She reheats yesterday's pasta and winds down quietly at home, savoring the meal and the silence.

diary: Rain all morning—the kind that makes you want to stay in bed with coffee, but I had to get moving. Took I-405 north instead of my usual bridge route; everyone else had the same idea, naturally. The restaurant was slammed for lunch, which meant good tips but also my feet hating me by hour four. Grabbed a sandwich in the back around three and finally had five minutes to breathe. Scrolled through some new jokes I've been workshopping—there's this bit about dating apps and commitment issues that might actually land. Dinner service was the usual controlled chaos. By the time I got home, I just heated up yesterday's mushroom pasta and poured wine. The rain had stopped by then. Sitting on my couch now, half-watching my reflection in the dark window, feeling that specific exhaustion where your body's done but your mind's still spinning with material.
thought_process: The bridge closure messed with everyone's morning, but I planned around it—no point stressing over what I can't control. Work was physically demanding, so I stayed focused on execution rather than overthinking the crowd. That break felt crucial; I needed to remind myself why I'm doing the day job in the first place. The new material is starting to feel sharper, and having even ten minutes to sit with those notes recharged something. By evening, I was running on fumes, so I kept dinner simple and low-key. The rain setting the mood helped—something about a grey day makes comedy feel more honest, more necessary.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
